question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that loanToken charges a transfer fee F on every transfer, trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets) → bundler receives maxBuyerAssets-F → forceApproveMax → MIDNIGHT.take pulls filledBuyerAssets from bundler → safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) where the refund amount exceeds actual bundler balance, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow revert freezing the transaction or silent drain of residual bundler balance from prior users? Proof idea: deploy FOT token with 1% fee, fuzz maxBuyerAssets and filledBuyerAssets, assert bundler loanToken balance >= computed refund amount after every take loop.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that loanToken charges fee F and referralFeePct > 0, trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, targetBuyerAssets) → bundler receives targetBuyerAssets-F → referralFeeAssets = targetBuyerAssets*referralFeePct/WAD → targetFilledBuyerAssets = targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets → MIDNIGHT.take pulls targetFilledBuyerAssets from bundler → if targetFilledBuyerAssets > targetBuyerAssets-F the pull consumes residual balance from prior users, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover all withdrawable assets, causing scoped impact: silent theft of other users' residual loan token balance left in the bundler? Proof idea: seed bundler with 1 wei residual, call with FOT token where fee > referralFeeAssets, assert bundler balance post-call is negative delta relative to pre-call.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that collateralToken charges fee F on transferFrom, trigger the sequence pullToken(collateralToken, msg.sender, assets) → bundler receives assets-F → forceApproveMax(collateralToken, MIDNIGHT) → MIDNIGHT.supplyCollateral(market, idx, assets, taker) where MIDNIGHT pulls assets from bundler but bundler only holds assets-F, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas, causing scoped impact: MIDNIGHT credits taker with assets collateral units while only receiving assets-F tokens, creating phantom collateral that inflates borrowing capacity? Proof idea: deploy FOT collateral token, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, assert MIDNIGHT.collateral(id, taker, idx) == actual tokens received by MIDNIGHT.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token as the sell-side asset under the precondition that loanToken charges fee F on transfer from MIDNIGHT to address(this), trigger the sequence MIDNIGHT.take sends filledSellerAssets to address(this) → bundler receives filledSellerAssets-F → safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) → safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, targetSellerAssets) where referralFeeAssets + targetSellerAssets == filledSellerAssets > bundler actual balance, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover all claimable assets, causing scoped impact: revert freezing the transaction after collateral has already been supplied and debt incurred, leaving taker with debt but no loan proceeds? Proof idea: deploy FOT loan token, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget, assert tx reverts after MIDNIGHT.supplyCollateral succeeds, verify taker has debt with no received assets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that loanToken charges fee F and referralFeePct is small (referralFeeAssets < F), trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, assets) → bundler receives assets-F → units = assets - referralFeeAssets → MIDNIGHT.repay(market, units, onBehalf) where MIDNIGHT pulls units from bundler but bundler only holds assets-F < units, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas, causing scoped impact: repay call consumes residual bundler balance from prior users or reverts, leaving borrower's debt unreduced while assets are lost? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [0, fee_rate), deploy FOT token, assert MIDNIGHT.debtOf(id, onBehalf) decreases by exactly units and bundler balance is non-negative post-call.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that loanToken charges fee F on transfer from MIDNIGHT to address(this) during take, trigger the sequence MIDNIGHT.take(offer, ..., taker, address(this), ...) → MIDNIGHT sends filledSellerAssets to bundler → bundler receives filledSellerAssets-F → referralFeeAssets = filledSellerAssets*referralFeePct/WAD → require(filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets >= minSellerAssets) passes → safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets) fails because bundler only holds filledSellerAssets-F, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover withdrawable assets, causing scoped impact: transaction reverts after debt is created, leaving taker with debt and no loan proceeds (fund freeze)? Proof idea: set minSellerAssets just below filledSellerAssets-referralFeeAssets, use FOT loan token, assert revert occurs after take succeeds.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: forceApproveMax / safeApprove] Can an unprivileged market creator deploying a token that returns false on approve(spender, type(uint256).max) under the precondition that the token's approve function returns false instead of reverting, trigger the sequence forceApproveMax(loanToken, MIDNIGHT) → safeApprove(token, MIDNIGHT, 0) succeeds → safeApprove(token, MIDNIGHT, type(uint256).max) → token.call returns (true, abi.encode(false)) → require(returndata.length == 0 || abi.decode(returndata, (bool)), ApproveReturnedFalse()) reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: all five bundler functions permanently revert for this token, freezing any user who attempts to use the bundler with this loan or collateral token? Proof idea: deploy mock token returning false on approve(max), call any bundler function, assert ApproveReturnedFalse() is thrown.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: forceApproveMax / safeApprove] Can an unprivileged market creator deploying a USDT-like token that reverts on approve(spender, nonzero) when current allowance is nonzero under the precondition that bundler already has allowance > 0 but < type(uint96).max/2 for MIDNIGHT, trigger the sequence forceApproveMax → allowance check fails threshold → safeApprove(token, MIDNIGHT, 0) succeeds → safeApprove(token, MIDNIGHT, type(uint256).max) → token reverts because it does not allow approve(nonzero) after approve(0) in same call context, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: DoS on all bundler functions for USDT-like tokens when allowance is in the intermediate range? Proof idea: set bundler allowance to type(uint96).max/2 - 1, call forceApproveMax, assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as referralFeeRecipient deploying a contract that reverts on ERC20 token receipt (e.g., ERC777 tokensReceived hook reverts) under the precondition that referralFeeAssets > 0, trigger the sequence all takes succeed → filledUnits == targetUnits → collateralWithdrawals execute → safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) → referralFeeRecipient.call reverts → entire transaction reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: taker cannot complete buy+withdraw in a single bundle because a malicious referralFeeRecipient can always cause revert, freezing the atomic operation? Proof idea: deploy reverting ERC777 receiver as referralFeeRecipient, call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct > 0, assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as receiver deploying a contract that reverts on ERC20 receipt under the precondition that filledSellerAssets > referralFeeAssets, trigger the sequence collateral supplied → takes succeed → safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) succeeds → safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets) → receiver reverts → entire transaction reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: taker incurs debt and supplies collateral but cannot receive loan proceeds because receiver is a reverting contract, leaving taker with debt and no assets? Proof idea: deploy reverting receiver, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, assert revert after MIDNIGHT state changes.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as msg.sender deploying an ERC777 token as loanToken with a tokensReceived hook that reenters buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral under the precondition that bundler has no reentrancy guard and the reentrant call uses the bundler's existing token balance, trigger the sequence outer call: pullToken pulls maxBuyerAssets → forceApproveMax → take loop → safeTransfer to referralFeeRecipient triggers tokensReceived → reentrant call: pullToken pulls another maxBuyerAssets → forceApproveMax (already approved) → take loop uses bundler balance from outer call, violating the invariant that multicall/callback/reentrancy must not break invariants that hold for single calls, causing scoped impact: reentrant call consumes outer call's token balance, causing outer call's refund safeTransfer to fail or send zero? Proof idea: implement ERC777 tokensReceived hook in referralFeeRecipient that reenters bundler, assert outer call's msg.sender receives correct refund.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as msg.sender using an ERC777 collateral token with a tokensToSend hook that reenters supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget under the precondition that the hook fires during safeTransferFrom before bundler receives tokens, trigger the sequence outer pullToken fires tokensToSend → reentrant call starts → reentrant pullToken fires tokensToSend again → nested reentrancy → outer call's forceApproveMax and supplyCollateral execute with stale state, violating the invariant that callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: collateral supplied multiple times or double-counted in MIDNIGHT accounting? Proof idea: implement ERC777 tokensToSend hook that reenters bundler, assert MIDNIGHT.collateral(id, taker, idx) matches exactly one supply worth of tokens.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged maker deploying a callback contract that always reverts under the precondition that all offers in the takes[] array have offer.callback set to the reverting contract, trigger the sequence for each offer: try MIDNIGHT.take(...) → MIDNIGHT calls maker callback → callback reverts → MIDNIGHT.take reverts → catch {} swallows revert → filledUnits never reaches targetUnits → require(filledUnits == targetUnits, OutOfOffers()) reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: taker's transaction always reverts after gas expenditure because maker can grief any bundle by deploying a reverting callback? Proof idea: set all offer callbacks to reverting contract, call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral, assert OutOfOffers() revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker front-running a taker's transaction by exhausting all offers in the takes[] array under the precondition that all offers share a consumption group and the attacker fills them to maxUnits before the taker's tx lands, trigger the sequence ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits returns 0 for each offer → unitsToTake = min(targetUnits-filledUnits, takes[i].units, 0) = 0 → MIDNIGHT.take(offer, ..., 0, ...) either no-ops or reverts → catch {} swallows → filledUnits stays 0 → OutOfOffers() revert, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where attacker can always grief any bundle take by front-running offer exhaustion? Proof idea: exhaust all offers before calling bundler, assert OutOfOffers() revert with zero gas cost to attacker.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as msg.sender (authorized by taker) under the precondition that msg.sender != taker and IMidnight.isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) == true, trigger the sequence set referralFeePct = WAD-1 → referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(WAD-1, 1) → potential overflow in (filledBuyerAssets * (WAD-1)) before division in mulDivDown since mulDivDown uses plain (x*y)/d arithmetic, violating the invariant that user funds must not be stolen, causing scoped impact: arithmetic overflow reverts the transaction or referralFeeAssets exceeds filledBuyerAssets causing underflow in refund calculation? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD-1 with large filledBuyerAssets, assert (filledBuyerAssets * referralFeePct) does not overflow uint256 and refund is non-negative.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender authorized by taker under the precondition that msg.sender != taker and isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) == true, trigger the sequence set referralFeeRecipient = msg.sender and referralFeePct = WAD-1 → referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets*(WAD-1)/(WAD-(WAD-1)) = filledBuyerAssets*(WAD-1) → safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, referralFeeAssets) extracts nearly all of taker's loan assets, violating the invariant that user funds must not be stolen outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: authorized operator steals taker's loan assets via referral fee mechanism? Proof idea: authorize attacker, call with referralFeePct=WAD-1 and referralFeeRecipient=attacker, assert attacker receives more than filledBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender authorized by taker under the precondition that msg.sender != taker and isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) == true, trigger the sequence set collateralReceiver = msg.sender → withdrawCollateral(market, idx, assets, taker, msg.sender) → MIDNIGHT transfers taker's collateral to msg.sender, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be seized unless liquidation rules allow it, causing scoped impact: authorized operator redirects taker's collateral withdrawal to attacker's address without taker's per-action consent? Proof idea: authorize attacker, call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralReceiver=attacker, assert attacker receives taker's collateral.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender authorized by onBehalf under the precondition that msg.sender != onBehalf and isAuthorized(onBehalf, msg.sender) == true, trigger the sequence set collateralReceiver = msg.sender and referralFeeRecipient = msg.sender → pullToken pulls assets from msg.sender → repay reduces onBehalf's debt → withdrawCollateral sends onBehalf's collateral to msg.sender → safeTransfer sends referralFeeAssets to msg.sender, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be seized outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: authorized operator repays onBehalf's debt using own funds but redirects all collateral and referral fees to self? Proof idea: authorize attacker, call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralReceiver=attacker, assert attacker receives collateral.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: pullToken (PermitKind.ERC2612)] Can an unprivileged front-runner under the precondition that a taker submits a bundle with PermitKind.ERC2612 and has not pre-approved the bundler, trigger the sequence front-runner calls IERC20Permit.permit(from, bundler, amount, deadline, v, r, s) consuming the permit → taker's tx lands → try IERC20Permit.permit(...) {} catch {} silently swallows the AlreadyConsumed revert → SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(token, from, address(this), amount) → reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse or insufficient allowance, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: front-runner can always grief any ERC2612-permit-based bundle by consuming the permit first, causing DoS? Proof idea: consume permit in same block before bundler tx, assert bundler tx reverts at safeTransferFrom.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: pullToken (PermitKind.Permit2)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that the Permit2 signature authorizes exactly amount tokens and the loan token is fee-on-transfer, trigger the sequence IPermit2.permitTransferFrom transfers amount from user to bundler → token charges fee F → bundler receives amount-F → forceApproveMax → MIDNIGHT.take pulls filledBuyerAssets from bundler where filledBuyerAssets may exceed amount-F, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas, causing scoped impact: MIDNIGHT pulls more than bundler holds, consuming residual balance from prior users or reverting mid-take? Proof idea: use Permit2 with FOT token, assert bundler balance after pullToken equals amount-F and MIDNIGHT only receives amount-F.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits returns units U such that the actual sellerAssets returned by MIDNIGHT.take for U units is strictly greater than targetFilledSellerAssets - filledSellerAssets (due to rounding), trigger the sequence filledSellerAssets accumulates to value > targetFilledSellerAssets → require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets, OutOfOffers()) reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: taker's collateral is already supplied and debt incurred but the bundle always reverts due to rounding mismatch between sellerAssetsToUnits inverse and MIDNIGHT's forward computation? Proof idea: fuzz tick, settlementFee, and targetSellerAssets near rounding boundaries, assert sellerAssetsToUnits(sellerAssetsToUnits^-1(x)) == x.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits returns units U such that MIDNIGHT.take for U units yields buyerAssets strictly greater than targetFilledBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets (rounding up in mulDivUp path for buy offers), trigger the sequence filledBuyerAssets accumulates to value > targetFilledBuyerAssets → require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets, OutOfOffers()) reverts, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: taker's targetBuyerAssets are pulled but the bundle always reverts due to rounding asymmetry between buyerAssetsToUnits and MIDNIGHT's take computation? Proof idea: fuzz offer.buy=true with mulDivUp path, assert buyerAssetsToUnits inverse is exact.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that takes[] array is empty (takes.length == 0), trigger the sequence takes[0].offer.market.loanToken → array index out of bounds panic (Solidity 0x32 panic), violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: any caller passing empty takes[] causes immediate panic revert, and if loanTokenPermit was already consumed by a prior call in a multicall context, the permit is wasted? Proof idea: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with takes=[], assert panic revert before any state change.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex is out of bounds for market.collateralParams.length, trigger the sequence market.collateralParams[collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex] → array index out of bounds panic, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: panic revert after prior collateral supplies in the same loop have already transferred tokens to the bundler but before supplyCollateral is called, leaving tokens stranded in the bundler? Proof idea: set collateralIndex = market.collateralParams.length, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, assert tokens are not stranded in bundler.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the loan token is a rebasing token (e.g., stETH) whose balance increases between pullToken and the refund safeTransfer, trigger the sequence pullToken pulls maxBuyerAssets → token rebases upward → bundler balance increases → refund safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) sends nominal amount → bundler retains rebase gain as stranded residual, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover withdrawable assets, causing scoped impact: rebase gains accumulate in bundler as unclaimable residual, effectively stealing yield from users? Proof idea: use rebasing token, warp time between pullToken and refund, assert bundler balance is zero after refund.
